sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Carpenter Creek: Open year around. Daily: 2 trout. Possession: 4 trout. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Carpenter Canyon Creek — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Carpenter Canyon Creek is located in the Southern region of Nevada in Clark County and is a creek or stream in the Spring Mountains; recently stocked rainbow trout respond well to a wide variety of baits, lures and flies.

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

regulations.humanSummary: Carpenter Creek is open to fishing year-round.

parameters.counting_rule: Limit applies specifically to trout (the only game fish present); 2 trout daily, 4 trout in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit 2 trout; possession limit 4 trout on Carpenter Creek.

parameters.note: Fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night on Carpenter Creek, per NDOW's Clark County regulation table.

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night on Carpenter Creek.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
